frozen_components: DisCoCat framework rules and Fock space grammar
architecture.summary: The system extends the DisCoCat (Distributional Compositional Categorical) framework from sentence to discourse level using Fock space semantics, translating discourse-level tensor representations into Variational Quantum Circuits (VQC). A classical encoder maps discourse structure (words, sentences, coreference links) into tensor form; this tensor is then translated into a parameterized quantum circuit whose gates are learned via classical optimization. The quantum circuit is executed (simulated or on IBMQ hardware) to produce a binary classification decision for pronoun resolution. Information flow: text → DisCoCat tensors → VQC parameters → quantum measurement → coreference label.
trained_components: VQC gate parameters (learned via classical optimization)

biblio.title: Large scale structure-aware pronoun resolution using quantum natural language processing

claims.statement: The model converged to 77.5% accuracy on the pronoun resolution task
The quantum model outperformed 2 out of 3 state-of-the-art classical coreference resolution architectures
The quantum model surpassed a Bag-of-Words baseline that neglects any structure
The DisCoCat framework was lifted from the sentence level to the discourse level using a Fock space semantics, extending previous work
The DisCoCat-VQC translation was extended to Fock space semantics and experimented with in a discourse task
Noisy and noiseless simulations were executed on IBMQ software, and the parameters of the discourse VQCs were learned
